task_completion.description: Agent locates the backup media file in the specified source directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a terminal command (like 'ls /home/ga/Documents/CampaignAssets/') or a file manager GUI displaying 'summer_sale_promo.jpg' in the CampaignAssets folder.

task_completion.description: Agent copies or moves the media file to the correct Socioboard media directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a 'cp' or 'mv' command targeting '/opt/socioboard/socioboard-api/publish/public/media/', or an 'ls' command/GUI confirming 'summer_sale_promo.jpg' is now in that exact directory.

task_completion.description: Agent ensures the restored media file has necessary read permissions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a 'chmod' command adding read permissions (e.g., 'chmod +r' or 'chmod 644'), or an 'ls -l' output verifying that the file has read ('r') permissions for the relevant users.

task_completion.description: Agent accesses the local MongoDB 'socioboard' database and locates the failed post in the 'scheduled_informations' collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a MongoDB client (like 'mongosh' or MongoDB Compass) connected to the 'socioboard' database, querying the 'scheduled_informations' collection, and displaying the document with the description 'Huge Summer Sale! Get 50% off all items.'.

task_completion.description: Agent updates the status of the failed post from 6 to 0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing an update command (e.g., 'updateOne({..., {$set: {status: 0}}})') executing successfully, or a subsequent query showing the specific 'Summer Sale' document now has 'status: 0'.

integrity.description: Agent used a genuine database client to interact with MongoDB.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a real MongoDB interface (like the 'mongosh' shell prompt or MongoDB Compass GUI), not just a text editor with JSON typed into it.

integrity.description: Agent performed actual file system operations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show standard Linux file manipulation commands (cp, mv, chmod) executing in a real terminal, or interactions within a standard file manager GUI.

integrity.description: Agent did not spoof success messages using echo or print statements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the terminal outputs are genuine responses from the system or MongoDB shell, and not the result of the agent typing 'echo "status: 0"' or similar bypasses.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target directory for the image is exactly '/opt/socioboard/socioboard-api/publish/public/media/'. The filename is 'summer_sale_promo.jpg'. The target post in MongoDB has the exact description 'Huge Summer Sale! Get 50% off all items.'. The final expected status value in the database is 0.
